source: Bootstrapping Recommendations at Chrome Web Store

concepts.finding: Google Chrome Web Store uses bootstrapping for new extensions with no interaction history. Cold-start recommendations via content-based similarity (extension description, category, developer). As interactions accumulate, transitions to collaborative filtering. Key insight: threshold-based transition from content-based to CF as interaction count increases.
concepts.implication: Cold-start keyword clustering: bootstrap with content features (keyword text, entity type, language), then transition to co-occurrence-based embeddings as frequency increases. Threshold determines when keyword has enough context for reliable intent assignment.
concepts.open_question: What interaction threshold marks the transition from content-based to context-based keyword intent prediction?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A hybrid keyword intent model (content-based for rare keywords, embedding-based for frequent ones) outperforms a single-model approach on total keyword coverage and accuracy.
